element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns to coordinate with R.O.B.'s slow mechanical movements; requires planning moves ahead
Early puzzles teach basic stacking before R.O.B. integration complexity increases
Player must anticipate R.O.B.'s deliberate pace and align eye-tracking for input reception

typical_tension_triggers: Need to plan multiple moves ahead for R.O.B. coordination
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Deliberately slow; R.O.B.'s mechanical limitations create extended puzzle solve time; described as 'watching grass grow'

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to basic stacking mechanics; simple R.O.B. coordination
notable_spikes: R.O.B. physical limitations (slow movement, eye-tracking sensitivity) create difficulty independent of puzzle design

notable_level_patterns.description: Player must think 'two or three moves ahead' to allow R.O.B. sufficient time to execute block manipulations; combines puzzle planning with real-time robot synchronization
Difficulty arises from R.O.B.'s inherent hardware limitations (slow speed, eye-tracking misalignment requiring repositioning) rather than puzzle complexity alone

sources_used: wikipedia - R.O.B. article (Hardware section describing R.O.B. mechanics and game design constraints)
tcrf - Stack-Up page (Developer credits and technical details)
notes_on_data_quality: Tier T3 (qualitative/empirical). Limited gameplay documentation available; analysis based on R.O.B. hardware capabilities and designer recollections of robot behavior rather than detailed level design documentation. Specific level sequences, room layouts, and progression metrics not documented in provided sources.
